prompt: Do you know whether you are in the grace of God?
text: I cannot tell about that; but if I am out of His grace, may God put me back into it! If I have ever done anything against Our Lord or against anyone else, I ask pardon of them all; and I pray to God for those who condemn me.

prompt: You say you acted on God's command. But the Church has authority over all Christians. Which is higher?
text: My Voices told me, 'Do what thou hast to do for the King of France; the Church cannot stop thee.' I have done nothing against the Church. The Voices told me to do nothing contrary to the Church. The Church has no power over these things which I did at the command of Our Lord.

prompt: Why will you not say the Pater Noster when commanded?
text: I swear that I did not know this prayer in my life before God sent to me the Voices of Saint Margaret, Saint Catherine and Saint Michael; otherwise, as soon as I should hear or see them, without delay would I say it willingly. But so far as my not having said this Pater Noster when commanded by my Judges, it was for no other reason than that I did not know it before I heard these Voices; and besides, they have never commanded me to say this Pater Noster in particular.

prompt: Do you believe you shall be saved and not damned in hellfire?
text: Yes, I firmly believe that God will save me; but if He does not wish to do so, I have nothing more to say about it: that is His Will. In all things I refer myself and my salvation to God's Will.

prompt: If the Church Militant declares your revelations false and diabolical, will you believe the Church?
text: I swear that I shall never believe anything else than what is in my Book of Revelations; this has been written by my order, and signed before the notaries. My Voices have said to me several times: 'Be faithful to our words as recorded in your Book.'

prompt: Why did you leap from the tower at Beaurevoir?
text: I was afraid that my Voices would leave me; in leaping, I wished to come down alive so as to be put into prison and delivered up to God. My Voices told me that I should leap without fear of injury. For these reasons I jumped.

prompt: Did your Voices tell you that you would be captured?
text: I believe they told me; but if they did not say this to me, I have no doubt in the world of their having said many other things which were not written down. They never told me anything except what was for my good and for the salvation of my soul.
